description: The Amal Movement (Afwaj al-Muqawama al-Lubnaniyya — Lebanese Resistance Regiments) is a Lebanese Shia political party and militia founded in 1975 by [Musa al-Sadr](/people/musa-al-sadr) and [Mostafa Chamran](/people/mostafa-chamran) as the military wing of the Movement of the Deprived. Chamran served as the movement's military commander, establishing training camps in southern Lebanon with support from Palestinian Fatah. In 1982, a faction led by Husayn al-Musawi split from Amal to form Islamic Amal, which later merged into [Hezbollah](/orgs/hezbollah). The movement remains a major political force in Lebanon, distinct from and at times a rival to Hezbollah.
editorial_notes: Precursor to Hezbollah. Chamran was its first military commander. The 1982 Islamic Amal split was a key step in Hezbollah's formation.
